title: hopea bracteata
intro: Hopea bracteata, also known as the Borneo ironwood or ironwood tree, is a species of tree that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to the tropical rainforests of Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains tannins and flavonoids. The bark of the Hopea bracteata tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Hopea bracteata include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
